commit_msg: docs(haiku-director): add emoji guidance and html rendering for act bodies
author: Omkar
title: CASE FILE — The Manual of Sins
subtitle: He wrote down exactly how to build the machine, step by step, so others could follow.
when_where: 🕐 1:46 PM on main, the hour when documentation feels like virtue. The office was quiet—too quiet—the kind of silence that comes after you've already said too much. 📋 Omkar sat at his terminal, not writing code anymore, but *explaining* code, turning the invisible crime into a manual anyone could read.
who_whom: 🔍 The haiku-director system was his creation, his confession, his blueprint for chaos. Now he was annotating it—emoji guidance, HTML rendering, every technical detail exposed under fluorescent light. 🎭 He wasn't hiding anymore. He was teaching.
what_why: 📝 Every line of documentation was a breadcrumb leading back to him. He added emoji guidance to make the act bodies *cinematic*, as if packaging the crime in prettier paper made it less of a crime. 🧠 The HTML rendering instructions were meticulous—he wanted others to see it the way he saw it, to feel the noir weight of every commit. 💀 Documentation is confession with a footnote. He had just written his own indictment and filed it under 'helpful.'
verdict: He didn't confess to the crime; he published a how-to guide.
